an3.65:1.2: at one time the _Bhagavā_, while wandering on tour among the Kosalans together with a large community of *bhikkhus*, arrived at a town of the Kālāmas called Kesamutti.
an3.65:1.4: 'The ascetic Gotama, a Sakyan son gone forth from the Sakyan clan, has arrived at Kesamutti.
an3.65:1.5: And this good reputation has spread about the Blessed Gotama:
an3.65:1.7: and indeed *sādhu* is the sight of such *arahants*.'
an3.65:11.1: 'This hateful person, Kālāmas, overcome by hatred, with mind [*citta*] consumed, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another's wife, speaks falsely, and induces others to do the same — which leads to his harm and suffering for a long time.'
an3.65:13.2: when delusion arises within a person, does it arise for his benefit or for his harm?'
an3.65:15.1: 'This deluded person, Kālāmas, overcome by delusion, with mind [*citta*] consumed, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another's wife, speaks falsely, and induces others to do the same — which leads to his harm and suffering for a long time.'
an3.65:17.1: 'What do you think, Kālāmas — are these *dhammas* wholesome or unwholesome?'
an3.65:2.1: Then the Kālāmas of Kesamutti approached the _Bhagavā_. Having approached, some paid homage to the _Bhagavā_ and sat down to one side; some exchanged greetings with the _Bhagavā_, and having exchanged courteous and friendly talk, sat down to one side; some directed their joined hands toward the _Bhagavā_ and sat down to one side; some announced their name and clan and sat down to one side; and some sat down to one side in silence. Seated to one side, the Kālāmas of Kesamutti said this to the _Bhagavā_:
an3.65:21.1: 'Censured by the wise or praised by the wise?'
an3.65:23.1: 'When undertaken and practiced, do they lead to harm and suffering, or not?
an3.65:23.2: What is your view on this?'
an3.65:24.1: 'When undertaken and practiced, *bhante*, they lead to harm and suffering.
an3.65:24.2: That is our view on this.'
an3.65:25.3: Do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by mere reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on reasons, do not go by acceptance through deliberation on views, do not go by the speaker's apparent ability, do not go by thinking 'this ascetic is our teacher.'
an3.65:25.5: 'These *dhammas* are unwholesome, these *dhammas* are blameworthy, these *dhammas* are censured by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and practiced, lead to harm and suffering — then, Kālāmas, you should abandon them' —
an3.65:25.6: what was said was said with reference to this.
an3.65:26.1: Come, Kālāmas. Do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by mere reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on reasons, do not go by acceptance through deliberation on views, do not go by the speaker's apparent ability, do not go by thinking 'this ascetic is our teacher.'
an3.65:26.3: 'These *dhammas* are wholesome, these *dhammas* are blameless, these *dhammas* are praised by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and practiced, lead to benefit and happiness' — then, Kālāmas, you should undertake them and live accordingly.
an3.65:27.2: when non-greed arises within a person, does it arise for his benefit or for his harm?'
an3.65:29.1: 'This non-greedy person, Kālāmas, not overcome by greed, with mind [*citta*] not consumed, does not kill living beings, does not take what is not given, does not go to another's wife, does not speak falsely, and does not induce others to do the same — which leads to his benefit and happiness for a long time.'
an3.65:3.1: 'There are, *bhante*, some ascetics and brahmins who come to Kesamutti.
an3.65:3.2: They expound and illuminate only their own doctrine, while they disparage, scorn, deprecate, and dismiss the doctrines of others.
an3.65:3.3: And there are other ascetics and brahmins, *bhante*, who also come to Kesamutti.
an3.65:3.4: They too expound and illuminate only their own doctrine, while they disparage, scorn, deprecate, and dismiss the doctrines of others.
an3.65:3.5: We, *bhante*, are thus left with doubt and uncertainty about them:
an3.65:3.6: 'Which of these venerable ascetics and brahmins speaks truth, and which speaks falsely?'
an3.65:3.7: 'It is indeed fitting for you to doubt, Kālāmas; it is indeed fitting for you to be uncertain.
an3.65:3.8: Doubt has arisen in you about something that is genuinely doubtful.
an3.65:31.2: when non-hatred arises within a person, does it arise …
an3.65:31.3: when non-delusion arises within a person, does it arise …
an3.65:33.1: 'What do you think, Kālāmas — are these *dhammas* wholesome or unwholesome?'
an3.65:37.1: 'Censured by the wise or praised by the wise?'
an3.65:39.1: 'When undertaken and practiced, do they lead to benefit and happiness, or not?
an3.65:39.2: What is your view on this?'
an3.65:4.1: Come, Kālāmas. Do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by mere reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on reasons, do not go by acceptance through deliberation on views, do not go by the speaker's apparent ability, do not go by thinking 'this ascetic is our teacher.'
an3.65:4.3: 'These *dhammas* are unwholesome, these *dhammas* are blameworthy, these *dhammas* are censured by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and practiced, lead to harm and suffering' — then, Kālāmas, you should abandon them.
an3.65:40.1: 'When undertaken and practiced, *bhante*, they lead to benefit and happiness.
an3.65:40.2: That is our view on this.'
an3.65:41.3: Do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by mere reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on reasons, do not go by acceptance through deliberation on views, do not go by the speaker's apparent ability, do not go by thinking 'this ascetic is our teacher.'
an3.65:41.5: these *dhammas* are wholesome, these *dhammas* are blameless, these *dhammas* are praised by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and practiced, lead to benefit and happiness — then, Kālāmas, you should undertake them and live accordingly' —
an3.65:41.6: what was said was said with reference to this.
an3.65:42.1: That noble disciple, Kālāmas, thus free from covetousness, free from ill-will, undeluded, fully aware [*sampajānā*], and with *sati*, dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], and likewise the second direction, the third, and the fourth; so above, below, across, everywhere, to all, in all its extent, pervading the entire world with a mind imbued with goodwill [*mettā*] — vast, exalted, measureless, without enmity, without ill-will.
an3.65:42.2: With a mind imbued with compassion [*karuṇā*] …
an3.65:42.3: with a mind imbued with gladness [*muditā*] …
an3.65:42.4: dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*], and likewise the second direction, the third, and the fourth; so above, below, across, everywhere, to all, in all its extent, pervading the entire world with a mind imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*] — vast, exalted, measureless, without enmity, without ill-will.
an3.65:43.1: That noble disciple, Kālāmas, has a mind [*citta*] thus free from enmity [*averacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus free from ill-will [*abyāpajjhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled [*asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus purified [*visuddhacitto*].
an3.65:43.2: For him, in this very life, four assurances are gained.
an3.65:43.3: 'If there is another world, and if there is fruit and result of *kamma* done well and done badly, then at the breaking up of the body, after death, I shall arise in a good destination, a heavenly world' — this is the first assurance gained by him.
an3.65:44.1: 'If there is no other world, and if there is no fruit and result of *kamma* done well and done badly, then right here in this very life I maintain myself free from enmity, free from ill-will, free from trouble, and happy' — this is the second assurance gained by him.
an3.65:45.1: 'If evil is done to one who does evil, then I, having no evil intention toward anyone —
an3.65:45.2: since I am not doing any evil deed, how can suffering touch me?' — this is the third assurance gained by him.
an3.65:46.1: 'If evil is not done to one who does evil, then I see myself purified in both respects' — this is the fourth assurance gained by him.
an3.65:47.1: That noble disciple, Kālāmas, has a mind [*citta*] thus free from enmity [*averacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus free from ill-will [*abyāpajjhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled [*asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus purified [*visuddhacitto*].
an3.65:47.2: For him, in this very life, these four assurances are gained.'
an3.65:48.1: 'So it is, _Bhagavā_, so it is, Well-Gone One.
an3.65:48.2: That noble disciple, *bhante*, has a mind [*citta*] thus free from enmity [*averacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus free from ill-will [*abyāpajjhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled [*asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus purified [*visuddhacitto*].
an3.65:48.3: For him, in this very life, four assurances are gained.
an3.65:48.4: 'If there is another world, and if there is fruit and result of *kamma* done well and done badly, then at the breaking up of the body, after death, I shall arise in a good destination, a heavenly world' — this is the first assurance gained by him.
an3.65:49.1: 'If there is no other world, and if there is no fruit and result of *kamma* done well and done badly, then right here in this very life I maintain myself free from enmity, free from ill-will, free from trouble, and happy' — this is the second assurance gained by him.
an3.65:5.2: when greed arises within a person, does it arise for his benefit or for his harm?'
an3.65:50.1: 'If evil is done to one who does evil, then I, having no evil intention toward anyone, since I am not doing any evil deed, how can suffering touch me?' — this is the third assurance gained by him.
an3.65:51.1: 'If evil is not done to one who does evil, then I see myself purified in both respects' — this is the fourth assurance gained by him.
an3.65:52.1: That noble disciple, *bhante*, has a mind [*citta*] thus free from enmity [*averacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus free from ill-will [*abyāpajjhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled [*asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto*], a mind [*citta*] thus purified [*visuddhacitto*].
an3.65:52.2: For him, in this very life, these four assurances are gained.
an3.65:53.2: we, *bhante*, go for refuge to the _Bhagavā_, and to the *dhamma* and the *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus*.
an3.65:53.3: May the _Bhagavā_ regard us, *bhante*, as lay followers who have gone for refuge from this day forth for as long as we live.'
an3.65:7.1: 'This greedy person, Kālāmas, overcome by greed, with mind [*citta*] consumed, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another's wife, speaks falsely, and induces others to do the same — which leads to his harm and suffering for a long time.'
an3.65:9.2: when hatred arises within a person, does it arise for his benefit or for his harm?'
